work.desc_title: Commentary on the first book from the five books of the Torah by John Chrysostom
creator: ark:/21198/s1x303

excerpt.as_written: القراه الاوله من سفر الكون [...ل] مَا خَلق الله السماءِ وَاَلاُرض
translation: First reading from the book of the cosmos: [...] God created the heavens and the earth…

excerpt.as_written: ويخرجوا فا على [اكسات] لقيامَه حَياه وَفَا على السيَّأت لقيامَه دينونهٍ
translation: And they shall depart with the [...] to the resurrection of life and with the wicked to the resurrection of judgment

note: This text is further subdivided with rubrication into quotations from the book of Genesis, first, and next, the commentary of Chrysostom, e.g. f. 9r.

excerpt.as_written: ربى والاهى اغفر لى انا الخاطى
translation: My Lord and my God, pardon me, a sinner...,

excerpt.as_written: كانت صعبه الشفا وادا قيست بمراحمه كانت قريبه الشفا وبربنا ينبغى المجد من الان وكل اوان والى دهر الداهرين
translation: It is difficult to perceive and if judged by his mercy, perception is near, and glory ought to be to our Lord from now, always, and forever

excerpt.as_written: يا اباى ولحباى القديسين الابس الروح هلمو افلتحتع اليوم كلنا وسمع ساع المعرفة الروحانيه
translation: Oh my father, and to my beloved, holy and clothed in the Spirit, come, let us all enjoy today the message of spiritual lore…

excerpt.as_written: وننال بذلك الحياه الدهريه فى ملكوه السما [بتحت] ورحمه الاهنا ومحلصنا وسيدنا ايسوع المسيح الذى له السبح والجلال والتسبحه والاكرام ولابيه ولروح قدسَة من الان والى [الدهر]
translation: By which we obtain eternal life in the kingdom of heaven [...] and the mercy of our God, our Savior, our Lord, Jesus Christ, to whom be the glory, majesty, praise, and honor and to the Father and the Holy Spirit from now to [eternity]

note: There are several rubricized subsections throughout.

work_wit.as_written: للسبح قوتى ورحابي قول القديس افرام على الاهات الذي سبحوا (f. 179r)

excerpt.as_written: قلبى يوجعنی فاتوجعوا معى يا ابها الاخوه وعبيد المسيح المباركين هلموا واسمعوا الان نفسى حزنيه وكلامي وجعه
translation: My heart pains me, so are you pained with me, oh fathers, brothers, and blessed servants of Christ. Come, hear now my sorrowful soul and painful words…

excerpt.as_written: ما دام لنا زمان لان هذا الحاكم الديان واقف على الابواب لتصنع هذا العالم البطال وننيح الى الادهار
translation: …as long as we have time, because the Ruler, the Judge, is standing at the gates to make this world nothing. Let us repose forever

work.desc_title: Mimar of the holy John Chrysostom
creator: ark:/21198/s1x303
ark:/21198/s1qp4m

excerpt.as_written: هات لنضاعف اليوم حرصنا زايدا على ما تقدم من ايامنا في الاقدام على الدخول في القول من تعليمنا
translation: Come, let us double our care today additionally over what has transpired in our days in daring to admit the teaching of what we have learned

excerpt.as_written: كما قال السليح مع الرب الي الابد مجتمعين اجتماعا لا يكون له فرقه ما دام الامد وما احتوي في الدهر الدايم سرمد انحر ولله الشكر دايما
translation: As the apostle says, with the Lord we will be together always and there will be no division throughout this time and what freedom eternity contains will be forever. And thanks be to God always

note: This text is purported to be a translation of Chrysostom by Ibrāhīm Ibn Yuḥannā al-Anṭākī

excerpt.as_written: قال الصوت الالهي تعالو الي يا معشر المتعوبين
translation: The divine voice says, “Come to me, you who are weary…”

excerpt.as_written: يا رب تغلب رحمتك شي الله قوي حكيم فاصي عدل
translation: Your mercy, oh Lord God who is strong, wise, [...], and just, prevails over everything

note: Several rubrics and decorative interlaces demarcate the following subsections: Ascetic Mimar by St. Basil on the Monk in the World and on Perfection, f. 189r; From a treatise of Basil, f. 196v; Unrubricated citation, f. 198r; From his third treatise, f. 199v; Another treatise, f. 201r

work.desc_title: Basil of Caesarea, Homilia in illud: Attende tibi ipsi (Deut. 15,9)
creator: ark:/21198/s1101c

excerpt.as_written: ان موهبه النطق واستعمالها مما وهبه الله لنا خالقنا
translation: The gift of speech and its use are those things which God, our Creator, has given to us…

excerpt.as_written: وكان ذلك منك كايكون من حضور ست عفيفه تحصر فتسكن جليه
